title: Route Handler: static activity feed JSON fixture (edge)
description: backend-agent implements a Next.js App Router Route Handler under projects/todo-reference/src/app that serves Social Activity Feed data from static fixture JSON (no SQLite social persistence). Handler lives at the edge/runtime boundary per Next conventions; response shape matches ui_spec.json from task 10. Justification: isolates HTTP contract, MIME/status behavior, and integration-test coverage for the feed API from React presentation in task 12.
acceptance: GET handler returns JSON derived from a checked-in fixture under the project (e.g. projects/todo-reference/src or a dedicated fixtures path documented in task notes) with stable schema aligned to 10-ui-refiner-stitch-ui-spec ui_spec feed projection.
No social domain aggregates or SQLite tables for posts/users; fixture-only MVP per architecture-brief.

Integration tests (container-only per testing skill) cover success response, Content-Type, and declared error/limit behavior if any; 100% line coverage for declared integration scope includes this handler module path as listed in quality-gate or brief.
Unit tests or pure parsers for fixture loading stay free of network; no mutable global singletons for DB (N/A for fixture file path — use explicit resolution).
Clean Architecture: no react imports; handler may call thin server utilities only; domain/application remain free of Next-specific imports in new modules.
implementation_notes: GET /api/activity-feed reads src/fixtures/activity-feed.json; ACTIVITY_FEED_FIXTURE_PATH override for tests; Dockerfile copies fixtures into runtime image.
